hero.title: About IKAMETI
hero.subtitle: Your trusted partner for residency in Turkey — we turn complex, confusing procedures into clear, guided steps.

story.title: Who We Are
story.p1: IKAMETI is a specialized residency-services team in Turkey. We exist because residency procedures are administratively complex, scattered and easy to get wrong — and one small mistake can cost you weeks or a rejection.
story.p2: We turn that process into clear, practical steps: you always know what you need, when to submit it, and how to avoid common mistakes. We don't just file paperwork — we manage your residency case from the first conversation to the final decision and beyond.

mission.title: Our Mission & Vision
mission.desc: To become the first reference for anyone settling in Turkey, through a clear, fast and reliable experience — delivering residency services built on clear steps, quick execution and the lowest possible rate of errors and rejections.

services.title: What We Do
services.subtitle: End-to-end residency support — every step handled by people who do this every day.

work.title: How We Work
work.subtitle: A simple, transparent process — from your first message to your residence card.

commit.title: Our Commitment
commit.desc: We are committed to accurate, up-to-date information, full privacy for your data, and an organized experience free of complexity.
commit.c1_d: We rely on official sources and keep up with the latest rule changes — so you never act on outdated information or rumors.
commit.c2_d: Your documents and personal details stay strictly confidential and are used only to complete your residency file.
commit.c3_d: We remove the complexity: clear steps, no confusing jargon, and a real person with you at every stage.

hero.title: Frequently Asked Questions
hero.subtitle: Everything you need to know about residency in Turkey. Get instant answers to common questions.

residency_q1.question: What are the main types of residency permits in Turkey?
residency_q1.intro: Turkey offers several residency options including:

residency_q2.question: How long does the residency application process take?
residency_q2.intro: Processing times vary by residency type:
residency_q2.option_1: Tourist Visa: On arrival (Schengen-free entry for 90 days)
residency_q2.option_2: Long-term Residency: 15-30 days for document processing
residency_q2.closing: Our service typically expedites this within 10-15 business days.

residency_q3.question: What documents do I need for residency application?
residency_q3.item_2: Proof of accommodation (rental contract or property deed)
residency_q3.item_3: Proof of income or financial means
residency_q3.closing: We provide a complete checklist customized to your specific situation.

residency_q4.question: Can I work while on residency permit?
residency_q4.intro: Work permissions depend on your residency type:
residency_q4.option_1: Tourist Residency: Work permission required separately
residency_q4.option_2: Student Residency: Limited work hours allowed (20 hours/week)
residency_q4.option_3: Real Estate Residency: Can establish business or become freelancer
residency_q4.option_4: Work Visa: Full employment rights with employer sponsorship
residency_q4.closing: We can guide you through obtaining work authorization alongside residency.

residency_q5.question: What is the cost of getting Turkish residency?
residency_q5.option_4: Service Fees: Our professional assistance typically ranges €500-1,500 depending on complexity
residency_q5.closing: Get a free consultation for exact pricing based on your situation.

work_q1.question: Can I start a business in Turkey on residency?
work_q1.intro: Yes! Many residency types allow business ownership:
work_q1.item_1: Register as a sole proprietor or establish a limited company
work_q1.item_2: Work visa holders can work for registered company
work_q1.item_3: Real estate visa holders can own and manage businesses
work_q1.item_4: Freelancers can operate as independent professionals
work_q1.closing: We assist with company registration and work permits.

work_q2.question: What is required to sponsor an employee in Turkey?
work_q2.intro: As an employer, you'll need to:
work_q2.item_5: Apply through Ministry of Labor and Social Security

work_q3.question: What are the taxation requirements for residents?
work_q3.intro: Taxation depends on your residency status:
work_q3.option_1: Limited Residents: Tax on Turkish income only
work_q3.option_2: Unlimited Residents: Global income subject to taxation
work_q3.item_3: Residents can benefit from tax treaties if applicable
work_q3.item_4: Annual tax filing required with Turkish authorities
work_q3.closing: We recommend consulting with a tax professional for personalized advice.

insurance_q1.question: Is health insurance mandatory for residency?
insurance_q1.item_1: Turkish Social Security Insurance (SGK) is mandatory for working residents
insurance_q1.item_2: Private insurance acceptable for non-working residents
insurance_q1.item_3: Minimum coverage must include emergency care
insurance_q1.closing: We help arrange appropriate insurance coverage.

insurance_q2.question: Can I buy property as a foreign resident?
insurance_q2.item_1: Foreign nationals can purchase property with proof of funds
insurance_q2.item_2: Property purchase can lead to residency qualification
insurance_q2.item_3: Some regions have restrictions on land (agricultural areas)
insurance_q2.item_4: Tax clearance required from country of origin
insurance_q2.item_5: Total property value no more than 30% of Ankara value for security
insurance_q2.closing: We connect you with trusted real estate partners.

insurance_q3.question: What happens if my residency expires?
insurance_q3.item_5: Limited tourists can leave and re-enter for another 90 days
insurance_q3.closing: We send renewal reminders and handle all paperwork.

insurance_q4.question: Can family members get residency based on my permit?
insurance_q4.item_1: Spouse and children can apply as dependents
insurance_q4.item_3: Financial support proof for dependents needed
insurance_q4.closing: Family reunion visas have special expedited processing.

insurance_q5.question: Is Turkish citizenship possible from residency?
insurance_q5.intro: Yes, citizenship is possible through residency:
insurance_q5.item_4: Property investment of €250K+ qualifies for 3-year path
insurance_q5.item_5: No renunciation of current nationality required
insurance_q5.closing: Dual citizenship is permitted under Turkish law.

cta.title: Didn't find your answer?
cta.description: Contact our expert team for personalized assistance with your residency questions.

tax_common.exempt_1: Student and humanitarian residencies are exempt from tax for all nationalities.
tax_common.exempt_2: Some nationalities are exempt from residency tax of all types, namely (Palestine - Syria - and some other non-Arab nationalities).
tax_common.exempt_3: In case of exemption from residency tax, only the residency card fee of 964 Turkish Lira is paid.

tax_q1.question: How much is the residency tax in Turkey?
tax_q1.intro: How much does a residence permit in Turkey cost? Residence permit fees (ikamet harcı) are based on the principle of reciprocity, so the residency fee depends on your nationality and on the type of short-term residence permit — tourist, real estate, family or medical. The fees below are valid from 01/01/2026, as determined by the General Communiqué on the Fees Law published in the Official Gazette No. 33124 dated 31 December 2025 by the Presidency of Migration Management (Göç İdaresi).
tax_q1.card_note_title: Residence card fee — applies to all nationalities:
tax_q1.card_note: For every nationality, a residence card fee of 964 TL is added to the residence permit application fee. Nationalities that are exempt from the residency fee pay only this 964 TL residence card fee.
tax_q1.standard_title: Standard residence permit fee — first month 25 USD · each additional month 5 USD
tax_q1.standard_desc: This standard residency fee applies to most nationalities, including the citizens of any country not listed in the special reciprocity groups below.
tax_q1.tiers_title: Reduced residence permit fees for specific nationalities:
tax_q1.tier_14: First month 14 USD · each additional month 3.5 USD
tax_q1.tier_9: First month 9 USD · each additional month 2.5 USD
tax_q1.tier_7: First month 7 USD · each additional month 1.5 USD
tax_q1.tier_5: First month 5 USD · each additional month 0.5 USD
tax_q1.exempt_note: Residency fee exemption: student residence permits and humanitarian residence permits — as well as certain nationalities — are exempt from the residence permit fee in Turkey; in that case only the 964 TL residence card fee is paid.
tax_q1.source: Official source — Presidency of Migration Management (goc.gov.tr):
tax_q1.closing: Need help calculating your exact residence permit fee or completing your ikamet application in Turkey? IKAMETI assists with residency permits, renewals, and the required documents for every nationality.

article_1.title: Turkish Residency Permit Guide
article_1.description: Complete guide to obtaining Turkish residency permits with step-by-step instructions.
article_1.author: Ahmed Al-Mansouri
article_1.content:
Introduction

Turkish residency permits are highly sought after by international professionals and investors. This comprehensive guide will walk you through the entire process, requirements, and timeline.

Types of Turkish Residency Permits

Turkey offers several types of residence permits designed for different purposes.

Short-Term Residence Permit (IKY)

Valid for 1 year and can be renewed. This is the most common type for expats starting their Turkish residency journey. It requires:

  • Valid passport
  • Health insurance
  • Certificate of residence
  • Clear criminal record

Long-Term Residence Permit (LKY)

Valid for 2 years and requires at least 1 year of continuous residence with a Short-Term permit. Requirements include the same documentation as above.

Application Process

The application process is straightforward and can be completed at your local Migration Office (Göç İdaresi). Prepare your documents, schedule an appointment, and submit your application.

Timeline

The typical processing time is 1-3 months, depending on your location and the volume of applications. You can extend your permit before it expires.

Cost

The cost varies by currency and exchange rate, typically ranging from 50-150 USD equivalent in Turkish Lira.

article_2.title: Finding Your Perfect Apartment in Istanbul
article_2.description: Expert tips and strategies for finding the ideal apartment in Istanbul's competitive housing market.
article_2.author: Fatima Hassan
article_2.content:
Introduction

Finding an apartment in Istanbul can be challenging but rewarding. With millions of residents and constant growth, the market is dynamic and competitive. This guide will help you navigate the process efficiently.

Best Neighborhoods for Expats

Istanbul offers diverse neighborhoods, each with unique characteristics. Here are top neighborhoods favored by expats:

Beyoğlu

The heart of modern Istanbul, known for its vibrant nightlife, restaurants, and cultural venues. Perfect for young professionals and those seeking an active lifestyle.

Beşiktaş

A more residential area with excellent transportation, upscale amenities, and strong expat community. Ideal for families and established professionals.

Kadıköy

Located on the Asian side, it's trendy, affordable, and very popular among expats. Great for those seeking a younger, artistic vibe.

Where to Search

Use multiple platforms for comprehensive search results: Sahibinden.com, Airbnb (for temporary rentals), Expatica forums, and local real estate agencies. Each has advantages.

What to Expect

Most landlords require first month, last month, and deposit. Apartments are typically unfurnished. Prices vary from 1,500 TL to 10,000+ TL depending on location and size.

Negotiation Tips

Don't accept the first offer. Landlords often expect negotiation. A 10-20% reduction from the asking price is common, especially for longer-term leases.

article_3.title: Understanding Turkish Labor Law
article_3.description: Essential information about employment contracts and worker rights in Turkey.
article_3.author: Mehmet Kaya
article_3.content:
Introduction

Understanding Turkish labor law is crucial for both employers and employees. Turkey has comprehensive labor regulations that protect workers' rights while allowing business flexibility.

Employment Contract Requirements

Every employment relationship in Turkey must be documented with a written contract. The contract must include:

  • Job title and responsibilities
  • Salary and payment terms (monthly minimum)
  • Working hours (maximum 45 hours/week)
  • Annual leave entitlement (minimum 2-2.5 days/month)
  • Probation period (maximum 2 months)
  • Termination conditions

Worker Rights

Turkish law guarantees fundamental rights to all employees regardless of contract type or status.

Minimum Wage

The monthly minimum wage is set annually by the government and applies to all workers. Employers cannot pay below this amount.

Working Hours

The standard working week is 45 hours distributed over 5 days. Overtime must be compensated at 1.5x the regular wage.

Leave and Holidays

Employees are entitled to paid annual leave. Public holidays are also paid days off.

Common Issues

If you face issues like non-payment of wages, wrongful termination, or unsafe working conditions, contact your local labor office (İş Gücü İdareliği).

article_4.title: Investment Opportunities in Turkey
article_4.description: Explore business and investment opportunities available for foreign investors in Turkey.
article_4.author: Sarah Thompson
article_4.content:
Introduction

Turkey presents compelling investment opportunities due to its growing economy, strategic location, and business-friendly policies. The government actively encourages foreign investment.

Key Investment Sectors

Turkey's economy offers diverse investment opportunities across multiple sectors.

Real Estate

The Turkish real estate market remains strong with consistent growth. Commercial and residential properties offer good returns, especially in Istanbul and Ankara.

Tourism

Turkey is a top tourist destination. Hotels, tour operators, and hospitality businesses show strong potential returns.

Technology

Istanbul's tech scene is booming with startups and innovation hubs. Tech investments are attracting venture capital globally.

Manufacturing

Strategic location between Europe and Asia makes Turkey attractive for manufacturing and export operations.

Investor Visa Requirements

Foreign investors can obtain residency permits by investing a minimum amount (typically $250,000 USD). Investment options include real estate purchase or business establishment.

Tax Benefits

Turkey offers tax incentives for foreign investors in designated sectors and regions. Consult with local tax advisors for specific benefits.

Registration Process

Establish your business through the Chamber of Commerce, obtain a tax ID, and register with social insurance. Most processes can be completed in 1-2 weeks.

article_5.title: Visa Types: Which One Do You Need?
article_5.description: Comprehensive overview of different Turkish visa types and how to choose the right one.
article_5.author: Elena Rodriguez
article_5.content:
Introduction

Turkey offers various visa options depending on your purpose of visit. Understanding the differences is crucial for a smooth entry into the country.

Tourist Visa (e-Visa)

The most common visa type for short-term tourism. Turkish e-Visas are valid for 90 days and can be obtained online in minutes.

Requirements

  • Valid passport (6+ months validity)
  • Credit/debit card for payment
  • Email address

Cost and Validity

The cost is approximately $20 USD. The visa is valid for single entry and covers stays of up to 90 days within a 180-day period.

Business Visa

For entrepreneurs and business professionals looking to establish business in Turkey.

Requirements

  • Invitation letter from Turkish company or organization
  • Proof of business registration in home country
  • Bank statements showing financial stability
  • Valid passport

Student Visa

For students enrolled in Turkish educational institutions. Duration matches your study program.

Requirements

  • Admission letter from Turkish university
  • Proof of financial support
  • Health insurance
  • Valid passport

Work Visa

Required if you're employed by a Turkish company. Your employer must provide sponsorship documentation.

Requirements

  • Work permit (obtained by employer)
  • Employment contract
  • Proof of qualifications
  • Valid passport

Long-Term Residency Visa

For those planning to stay long-term. Leads to residence permit eligibility.
